title: What Hospice Doesn't Cover (and How to Fill the Gaps)
summary: Hospice covers a great deal, but not everything — knowing the boundaries in advance prevents surprise gaps.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: What Hospice Doesn't Cover (and How to Fill the Gaps) | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Hospice covers a great deal, but not everything — knowing the boundaries in advance prevents surprise gaps.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Hospice generally covers medications, equipment, and services related to the terminal diagnosis.
Room and board in a facility (if not the hospice's own inpatient unit) is often not covered.
Treatments aimed at curing the terminal illness are typically outside hospice's scope, by definition.

paragraphs: Hospice (through Medicare's hospice benefit, for those enrolled) generally covers medications, equipment, and services directly related to the terminal diagnosis, along with the core visiting care team — but room and board costs at an assisted living or nursing facility are often billed separately, which can surprise families expecting fuller coverage.

Because hospice specifically shifts away from curative treatment for the terminal condition, any treatment aimed at curing that particular illness generally falls outside its coverage — though care for unrelated conditions may still continue.

nextSteps: Ask the hospice provider directly what is and isn't included in your specific situation.
Clarify facility room-and-board costs separately if applicable.
Ask about supplemental coverage options if a gap is identified.
disclaimer: General information, not financial advice — the hospice provider's admissions team can clarify exact coverage for your situation.

sources.name: National Hospice and Palliative Care Organization
